response.justification: The candidate is a strong generalist engineer with deep web, TypeScript/Node.js, and DLang systems experience, but the role's core mandate is 5–10 years of hands-on C++ on Windows plus SQL Server/Oracle and fixed-income or capital-markets domain knowledge. His C++ exposure is limited to a ~7-month stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and academic projects, his database background centers on MongoDB/MySQL rather than SQL Server/Oracle, and his only finance-adjacent role (RIDE Capital) was consumer investment tooling in Node/GraphQL, not capital markets or fixed income. The gap on all mandatory requirements makes him a poor match despite solid engineering fundamentals.
strengths: Strong software craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, pairing, trunk-based development
Proven low-latency systems experience (DLang, async I/O, millions of requests/sec)
Broad polyglot background with excellent communication and teaching record
concerns: C++ experience is brief (~7 months professionally, in 2014–2015) and not Windows-platform focused
No SQL Server or Oracle experience; database work is MongoDB/MySQL
No fixed income, derivatives, or capital-markets domain exposure

key_factors.factor: Strong general engineering and performance-systems fundamentals
